title: Polestar 7 To Be Produced In Europe Exiting China
text: Polestar decided to produce its Polestar 7 compact SUV in Europe rather than China, signaling a major strategic shift away from Chinese manufacturing. The decision reflects escalating EU-China trade tensions and Polestar's strategy to localize production for regional markets. The Polestar 7 will be manufactured at Volvo's facility in Slovakia, beginning in the second half of 2025. This represents Polestar's entry into the highly competitive and profitable compact SUV segment with European production as a foundation. European production reduces transportation costs, tariff exposure, and supply chain complexity. The strategy also enables Polestar to participate in European EV incentive programs and achieve carbon emission targets. The Polestar 7 targets affluent European buyers seeking premium electric SUVs with distinctive design and performance. Production in Slovakia leverages Volvo's manufacturing expertise and existing capacity. This European-first approach contrasts sharply with earlier assumptions of Chinese-dominant manufacturing. The pivot demonstrates management's responsiveness to geopolitical developments and tariff policies. By 2028, most Polestar vehicles will be manufactured outside China, with production spread across US, South Korea, and Europe based on regional demand patterns.
